channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: How the US and Israel are waging war on Iran’s medicines, vaccines
item.description: The Pasteur Institute of Iran is one of several healthcare facilities in the country to be targeted.

item.title: Over 100 US legal experts condemn strikes on Iran as possible ‘war crimes’
item.description: United States-based scholars sign open letter raising concerns about conduct, rhetoric during US-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: Kuwait desalination plant, oil refinery hit by missile and drone strikes
item.description: Twelve people were injured by falling debris in the UAE, fire at Abu Dhabi gas plant.

item.title: Iran condemns US-Israeli ‘moral collapse’ after attacks on civilian sites
item.description: Strikes damage or destroy one of Iran’s tallest bridges and a century-old medical research centre fighting diseases.

item.title: Iranian women footballers reveal ‘enormous pressure’ of the Asian Cup saga
item.description: Two Iranian players who sought asylum in Australia before changing their minds tell their story to Al Jazeera.

item.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 35 of US-Israeli attacks?
item.description: US and Israel hit century-old medical research centre and a bridge near Tehran.

item.title: How China’s ‘teapot’ refineries are cushioning it from Iran war oil crisis
item.description: Small independent refineries are helping China continue importing oil from Iran.

item.title: Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
item.description: Six weapons experts have contested the US claim that video evidence suggests an Iranian missile could have hit the hall.

item.title: International law experts allege violations in Iran war
item.description: The White House says it is making the entire region safer by eliminating short and long-term threats.

item.title: US jobs surge unexpectedly in March despite Iran war
item.description: Employers added 178,000 jobs, far more than had expected, the Labor Department says.
